why_this_artifact_exists: The rocWMMA selected-tile source-boundary branch was rejected because its producer is already too slow before dKV/dQ epilogues. This pins the replacement branch: keep the query-major dot schedule that is fast in Triton/TileLang, but consume source P/dP on chip instead of materializing slabs.

control_points.name: materialized_query_major_compact_micro_sink
control_points.read: Fast query-major dot-K producer floor with route-slot traversal and checksum only. This is the schedule family to preserve, but it is not source-boundary.

control_points.name: materialized_actual_li_tilelang_selected_limit_dkv_body
control_points.read: Current materialized-input actual-route TileLang dKV body timing. It proves the reduced dKV body can be in the right latency band if P/dP are already available.

control_points.name: source_recompute_pdp_sink
control_points.read: Recomputes P and scaled-dP once from source tensors, but only writes a checksum sink. It is useful as a source row-term gate, not a detachable production stage.

control_points.name: source_recompute_pdp_store
control_points.read: Writes a BF16 P/scaled-dP slab. Store cost is not the only issue; adding the existing selected-limit dKV consumer projects to about 15.599 ms before integration overhead.

control_points.name: source_pdp_store_then_dkv
control_points.read: Concrete two-kernel source P/dP slab pipeline. Rejected because the detached slab boundary erases the dKV body win.

control_points.name: source_chunked_grouped_recompute
control_points.read: Best legal slab-free grouped-D TileLang recompute point. Rejected because the accumulator/recompute shape is still far too expensive.

control_points.name: rocwmma_selected_tile_score_only
control_points.read: Source-boundary rocWMMA selected-tile score-page producer. Rejected because it is already slower than the full sparse-attention backward gate before contribution, dKV, or dQ.

closed_branches.branch: detached P/dP recompute or store before dKV
closed_branches.reason: Source row terms alone cost about 8.4-8.7 ms; adding a dKV consumer lands at about 15-16 ms and does not beat the 11.13 ms full-boundary gate.
It improves correctness semantics but loses query-major Q/dO reuse; legal target-shape points are tens of ms.
The producer is 21.46 ms score-only and 41.60 ms with contribution sink, so epilogue tuning cannot rescue it.

closed_branches.branch: late dKV-only TileLang replacement as e2e integration
closed_branches.reason: The dKV body is promising in isolation, but TorchTitan still pays materialized P/scaled-dP/selected-state bridge overhead before calling it.

next_kernel_contract.goal: source-boundary query-major sparse-MLA backward producer-consumer kernel
schedule_requirements: Preserve query-major Q/dO reuse like dot_k_compact_micro_sink, not group/panel-owned recompute.
Produce P/scaled-dP inside the same persistent or cooperative producer that consumes them.
Use local dQ accumulation or compact bounded dQ partials; do not selected-tile atomic-add dQ as the production schedule.
Use group-aware dKV segmented reduction before global writes, but only after the source producer is below the 8-11 ms band.
First gate should be producer-only or producer+checksum below 8 ms, then producer+dKV below the 11.13 ms full-boundary gate, before any TorchTitan e2e run.
preferred_implementation_surface: CK block-attention style producer if it can express route-gathered sparse K/V and custom epilogue
raw HIP persistent producer-consumer with MFMA/rocWMMA fragments if CK cannot express the sparse route epilogue
TileLang only if a primitive appears that keeps a persistent grouped accumulator without full active-row materialization

promotion_rule.single_gpu_latency: Do not promote to TorchTitan unless S4096 source-boundary sparse-attention backward latency beats the current 11.13 ms gate or clearly removes enough bridge overhead to improve full-boundary latency.
promotion_rule.e2e: Only run 4x/8x TFLOPs materialization after a latency candidate beats the current sparse-attention backward boundary or a MoE/MORI change is expected to move the sustained 64.91 TFLOPs/GPU baseline.
promotion_rule.memory: Record peak memory for every promoted run; no activation checkpointing plus expandable allocator is the current sustained e2e baseline.
